title: Analyze the Efficacy of SpeciesNet/MegaDetector on Your Dataset
text: first thing ’ll filter images multiple species present. done two reasons. First, ’s really difficult analyses use bounding box data multiple species one image: bounding box goes ? Second, SpeciesNet actually assign ID confidence boxes image, ’ll never get good results images. happens SpeciesNet usually set look highest confidence detection, assigns whatever thinks box contains boxes image. example , image “9487.jpg” ena24detection subset contains two cottontail rabbits american crow. three easily visible crow thing image, probably ’ve detected either crow bird. , one cottontails happened higher confidence MegaDetector ’s SpeciesNet used. Next, get SpeciesNet JSON file flatten table using megadet_flatten(). dataset many pictures chickens, SpeciesNet assigns typically red junglefowl rarely domestic chicken. want look IDed wrong later since really category, ’ll changing chicken records value. Now merge two dataframes. gives us table SpeciesNet said actual ID . row bounding box like specnet_data. IDs SpeciesNet use species, don’t need anything update values.
code: #Filter on if ; (seperator) is in species id_data <- ena24detection_subset[   stringr::str_detect(ena24detection_subset$species, ";", negate = TRUE), ]  paste0("New dataset has ", nrow(ena24detection_subset) - nrow(id_data), " fewer records.") |>    print() #> [1] "New dataset has 19 fewer records." #Get file path for JSON data json_file <- system.file(   "extdata",    "ena24subset_SpecNet_recognition.json",    package = "camRa" )  #Flatten to table specnet_data <- camRa::megadet_flatten(json_file)  #Update "domestic chicken" to be "red junglefowl" specnet_data$classification_category[   specnet_data$classification_category == "domestic chicken" ] <- "red junglefowl" #Merge data species_data <- dplyr::left_join(specnet_data, id_data, by = "file")  #Remove NA in species rows (these are images w/ 2+ species) species_data <- dplyr::filter(species_data, !is.na(species))

title: Analyze the Efficacy of SpeciesNet/MegaDetector on Your Dataset
text: first thing want see ’s relationship SpeciesNet MegaDetector confidence values. graph, ’ll see SpeciesNet tends high values, MegaDetector bit place, clusters low high values dataset. SpeciesNet mainly giving high values known phenomenon way build model. MegaDetector tends lot variance ’s values still cluster tested datasets.  Next, want see confidence related species ’re missing lot setting certain confidence thresholds. ’re going plot things actual ID given image, measure actual animal image. SpeciesNet, confidence , course, always high, two lowest confidence values belong images empty, ’s actually pretty good. empties included, may see consistently lower confidence ideal. MegaDetector, concern cutoffs. can see species detections 0.2 confidence cutoff currently suggested model version 5a want use MegaDetector remove empty images. species, loss might really matter, like white-tailed deer eastern U.S. incredibly abundant images lose using >0.2 likely wouldn’t matter wildlife activity metrics. hand, MegaDetector struggles chipmunks also relatively common across lot area. ’re mainly interested specific species, distribution good consider. bobcats species interest project, loss two bobcat images 25 acceptable? risk can’t take? 8.6% (23 vs. 25) increase bobcat images worth reviewing images, especially MegaDetector clustering values near 0 confidence?  Finally, want know SpeciesNet getting wrong. , ’m going use modified confusion matrix. Due large number taxonomic categories SpeciesNet actual IDs, modified plot just shows taxons kind mismatch removes matches. IDs almost correct, like saying bird species can identified mephitis species striped skunks. Overall, can see decent amount confusion within squirrels—cases model classified highly (says fox squirrel can’t determined) said sciurus species sciuridae can identified higher taxon. dataset, clear mis-identifications, good also expected cameras placed areas wildlife end near camera (buildings, facing wall) rather far away can make identification SpeciesNet worse.
code: ggplot(species_data, aes(x = detection_conf, y = classification_conf)) +   geom_point() +   theme_classic() +   labs(x = "MegaDetector Confidence", y = "SpeciesNet Confidence") ggplot(species_data, aes(x = detection_conf, y = classification_conf)) +   geom_point() +   theme_bw() +   facet_wrap(~species) +    geom_vline(xintercept = 0.2, linetype = "dashed") +   labs(x = "MegaDetector Confidence", y = "SpeciesNet Confidence") #Create matrix confusion_matrix <- table(   Predicted = species_data$classification_category,    Actual = species_data$species ) |> data.frame()  #Fix factor columns confusion_matrix$Predicted <- as.character(confusion_matrix$Predicted) confusion_matrix$Actual <- as.character(confusion_matrix$Actual)  #Filter to only non-zeros for  confusion_matrix <- dplyr::filter(confusion_matrix, Freq > 0 & Predicted != Actual) ggplot(data = confusion_matrix, aes(x = Predicted, y = Actual, fill = Freq)) +   geom_tile() +    geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +    theme_minimal() +    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +   labs(x = "Predicted (SpeciesNet)", y = "Actual", fill = "Count")

what: Provides Utilities for Managing Camera Trap Data
title: Provides Utilities for Managing Camera Trap Data
text: package currently pre-release. Information Vignettes official tests built package currently missing. camRa R package aimed providing utilities managing manipulating camera trap-related data. CamRa includes utilities help obtaining data Living Library Alexandria (LILA), summarizing updating MegaDetector/SpeciesNet detection files, general utilities camera trap image data.

title: Provides Utilities for Managing Camera Trap Data
text: Getting info LILA datasets downloading images LILA. Filtering, updating/merging classification names, converting JSON flat tables, easily grabbing values JSON files MegaDetector/SpeciesNet data. easy--use optical character recognition (OCR) implementation extract text (temperature date-time) camera trap images.

title: Provides Utilities for Managing Camera Trap Data
text: Expanded manipulation JSON data. Expanded OCR implementations ability train models. Functions manipulate actual images summarize aspects (measuring brightness/darkness calculating differences images) help eliminate empty “bad” images. Functions related Timelapse (image processing program). Simple implementations common annoying data processing tasks (TBD) vignettes! Test implementation (boring…).

title: Provides Utilities for Managing Camera Trap Data
text: camRa currently CRAN, can installed Github using one options : Don’t forget import !
code: #Depricated, use for older R versions devtools::install_github("oxyppgyn/camRa") #Most up-to-date method pak::pak("oxyppgyn/camRa") library(camRa)

title: Convert between BBox Formats — convert_bbox
text: Converts bounding box data three common formats: xyxy, xywh, centerwh. WARNING: function experimental, math behind conversions may wrong yet validated.

title: Convert between BBox Formats — convert_bbox
code: convert_bbox(   bbox,   from,   to,   image = NULL,   img_width = NULL,   img_height = NULL,   value_type = "absolute" )

title: Convert between BBox Formats — convert_bbox
text: bbox character vector. Bounding box values. , : character vector. Bounding box format convert . character vector. Bounding box format convert . Options: "xyxy", "xywh", "centerwh" image character magick-image. image extract width height . used converting value types (value_type opposite provided bounding box). Value type input bounding box inferred. Mutually exclusive img_width img_height. img_width, img_height numeric. width height image bounding box belongs . used converting value types (value_type opposite provided bounding box). Value type input bounding box inferred. Mutually exclusive mutually exclusive image. value_type character. Type values use converted bounding box measures. Either "absolute" pixel values "relative" scaled values.

Use NULL get text entire image. ... additional arguments passed OCR engine tesseract::tesseract().

what: Calculate Luminosity/Brightness of an Image — img_luminosity
title: Calculate Luminosity/Brightness of an Image — img_luminosity
text: Extracts luminance information specified colorspace returns appropriate channel.

title: Calculate Luminosity/Brightness of an Image — img_luminosity
code: img_luminosity(image, method = "grayscale", file = NULL, bbox = NULL)

title: Calculate Luminosity/Brightness of an Image — img_luminosity
text: image character magick-image. Image extract information . method character. method used calculate luminosity/brightness. Options "greyscale" "grayscale" get greyscale version RGB image, "LAB" extract lightness (L) channel LAB colorspace, "HSV" extract value (V) channel HVS colorspace, "YCbCr" luminance channel (Y) YCbCr color space, "YIQ" luma (Y) channel YIQ colorspace. file character. File path output image. bbox numeric vector. Bounding box values. Formatted xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax. Use convert_bbox() convert formats.

title: Calculate Luminosity/Brightness of an Image — img_luminosity
text: magick image.

title: Download LILA Image Files — LILA_download

title: Download LILA Image Files — LILA_download

title: Download LILA Image Files — LILA_download
text: dataset character. Name dataset download images . Can either short name standard name. See LILA_list_datasets(). files character character vector. Partial path(s) file name(s) images downloaded. Files may partial paths included (folder1/imgname.JPG) depending buckets organized. files stored "folder" S3, need file name extension. File names typically available JSON metadata files hosted LILA website via LILA_list_files(). dir character. Directory images saved. quiet boolean. information file downloads printed console.

title: Get Values from MegaDetector/SpeciesNet JSON Files — megadet_get_info
text: json character vector nested list object jsonlite::read_json(). JSON file loaded JSON data filter . key character, character vector, list, NULL. Key(s) /indices filter JSON data. first level keys, provide character key name. higher levels, provide vector keys order nested, c("info", "megadetector_version") get MegaDetector version. list keys indices can also used, list("images", 1) detection information first image file. NULL passed key, entire JSON file returned. file character. File write JSON data . Use NA skip writing file. validate_json boolean. JSON data formatted nested lists validated. can prevent unexpected errors parameter list, JSON may increase runtime.

text: json character vector nested list object jsonlite::read_json(). JSON file loaded JSON data filter . values_from character vector. vector classification names currently JSON updated. must unique values include names present file. values_to character vector. vector classification names reassign current classification names . Multiple copies name can present map values many--one. values_description character vector. vector classification descriptions reassign current classification descriptions . must unique values map onto values_from. Use NA skip updating descriptions instead remove file. file character. File write JSON data . Use NA skip writing file. validate_json boolean. JSON data formatted nested lists validated. can prevent unexpected errors parameter list, JSON may increase runtime.
